# Excel MCQ Bank - PG Program in Data Analytics & Gen AI

## Beginner
### Core MCQs (Concept + Tool-Usage)
Q1. Which statement best explains the role of Excel interface, ribbon, tabs, cell referencing (relative, absolute, mixed) in Excel for UK data analytics work? [Concept]
A. It improves reliability, interpretability, and auditability before decisions are made.
B. It is mainly a visual styling choice with no effect on analytical quality.
C. It guarantees that all source data is correct even when no validation is performed.
D. It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.
Correct answer: A
Explanation: Excel interface, ribbon, tabs, cell referencing (relative, absolute, mixed) matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

Q2. Which practical step best demonstrates correct use of Excel interface, ribbon, tabs, cell referencing (relative, absolute, mixed) in a workbook? [Tool-Usage]
A. Assume the default output is correct and skip all checks against the source.
B. Use the tool feature deliberately, check a small sample, and document any assumption in the workbook.
C. Change every field to text to avoid thinking about data types or aggregation.
D. Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.
Correct answer: B
Explanation: Correct use of Excel interface, ribbon, tabs, cell referencing (relative, absolute, mixed) means applying the right Excel capability and checking it against formula outputs, PivotTable totals, source row counts, and sample records. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

Q3. What is the strongest reason to understand Basic formulas: SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT, MAX, MIN before presenting analysis? [Concept]
A. It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.
B. It should be used only after the report has already been published.
C. It reduces ambiguity in definitions, calculations, and assumptions.
D. It is useful only for personal practice files and not for team analytics work.
Correct answer: C
Explanation: Basic formulas: SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT, MAX, MIN matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

Q4. Which workflow is the best way to use Basic formulas: SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT, MAX, MIN in production-style analysis? [Tool-Usage]
A. Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.
B. Build the answer manually and avoid leaving a repeatable calculation path.
C. Hide errors in the final output without checking why they occurred.
D. Start with the business requirement, use the correct feature, and test the output before sharing it.
Correct answer: D
Explanation: Correct use of Basic formulas: SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT, MAX, MIN means applying the right Excel capability and checking it against formula outputs, PivotTable totals, source row counts, and sample records. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

Q5. In a GBP 45,000+ analyst role, how should Basic formatting, number formats, borders be treated? [Concept]
A. It supports repeatable analysis rather than one-off manual judgement.
B. It is useful only for personal practice files and not for team analytics work.
C. It replaces the need to understand the business question.
D. It is relevant only when the dataset contains fewer than one hundred rows.
Correct answer: A
Explanation: Basic formatting, number formats, borders matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

Q6. How should Basic formatting, number formats, borders be used so the result can be checked and repeated? [Tool-Usage]
A. Hide errors in the final output without checking why they occurred.
B. Create the output, inspect edge cases, and confirm that the result answers the stakeholder question.
C. Prioritise cosmetic formatting before confirming the metric definition.
D. Share the output immediately and wait for stakeholders to find any issues.
Correct answer: B
Explanation: Correct use of Basic formatting, number formats, borders means applying the right Excel capability and checking it against formula outputs, PivotTable totals, source row counts, and sample records. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

Q7. Which statement best connects Sorting and filtering data to business decision-making? [Concept]
A. It is relevant only when the dataset contains fewer than one hundred rows.
B. It means the analyst no longer needs to document assumptions.
C. It improves reliability, interpretability, and auditability before decisions are made.
D. It is mainly a visual styling choice with no effect on analytical quality.
Correct answer: C
Explanation: Sorting and filtering data matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

Q8. What is the best operational habit when working with Sorting and filtering data? [Tool-Usage]
A. Share the output immediately and wait for stakeholders to find any issues.
B. Paste static values or screenshots so the result cannot be refreshed or audited.
C. Assume the default output is correct and skip all checks against the source.
D. Use the tool feature deliberately, check a small sample, and document any assumption in the workbook.
Correct answer: D
Explanation: Correct use of Sorting and filtering data means applying the right Excel capability and checking it against formula outputs, PivotTable totals, source row counts, and sample records. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

B. Build the answer manually and avoid leaving a repeatable calculation path.
C. Hide errors in the final output without checking why they occurred.
D. Start with the business requirement, use the correct feature, and test the output before sharing it.
Correct answer: D
Explanation: Correct use of Basic formulas: SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT, MAX, MIN means applying the right Excel capability and checking it against formula outputs, PivotTable totals, source row counts, and sample records. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

### Scenario-Based Questions - Beginner
Q1. You are working for a UK retail chain. A manager asks you to explain a small week-on-week KPI movement. The work involves Excel interface, ribbon, tabs, cell referencing (relative, absolute, mixed) in Excel. What should you do first? [Scenario]
A. Delete outliers or missing values before checking what they represent.
B. Clarify the metric, inspect the relevant fields, apply Excel interface, ribbon, tabs, cell referencing (relative, absolute, mixed) in a controlled workbook, and validate the result before sharing.
C. Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
D. Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.
Correct answer: B
Explanation: This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Excel interface, ribbon, tabs, cell referencing (relative, absolute, mixed), plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

Q2. A stakeholder at a London fintech team needs a quick but reliable answer to prepare a clean summary for a team meeting. The key skill is Basic formulas: SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT, MAX, MIN. What is the best next step? [Scenario]
A. Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
B. Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.
C. Check the business question first, use Basic formulas: SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT, MAX, MIN on the right data, and compare the output with a simple trusted total.
D. Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.
Correct answer: C
Explanation: This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Basic formulas: SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT, MAX, MIN, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

## Intermediate
### Core MCQs (Concept + Tool-Usage)
Q1. Which statement best explains the role of Logical functions: IF, AND, OR, Nested IF in Excel for UK data analytics work? [Concept]
A. It improves reliability, interpretability, and auditability before decisions are made.
B. It is mainly a visual styling choice with no effect on analytical quality.
C. It guarantees that all source data is correct even when no validation is performed.
D. It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.
Correct answer: A
Explanation: Logical functions: IF, AND, OR, Nested IF matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

Q2. Which practical step best demonstrates correct use of Logical functions: IF, AND, OR, Nested IF in a workbook? [Tool-Usage]
A. Assume the default output is correct and skip all checks against the source.
B. Use the tool feature deliberately, check a small sample, and document any assumption in the workbook.
C. Change every field to text to avoid thinking about data types or aggregation.
D. Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.
Correct answer: B
Explanation: Correct use of Logical functions: IF, AND, OR, Nested IF means applying the right Excel capability and checking it against formula outputs, PivotTable totals, source row counts, and sample records. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

Q3. What is the strongest reason to understand Lookup functions: VLOOKUP, HLOOKUP, INDEX-MATCH before presenting analysis? [Concept]
A. It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.
B. It should be used only after the report has already been published.
C. It reduces ambiguity in definitions, calculations, and assumptions.
D. It is useful only for personal practice files and not for team analytics work.
Correct answer: C
Explanation: Lookup functions: VLOOKUP, HLOOKUP, INDEX-MATCH matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

Q4. Which workflow is the best way to use Lookup functions: VLOOKUP, HLOOKUP, INDEX-MATCH in production-style analysis? [Tool-Usage]
A. Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.
B. Build the answer manually and avoid leaving a repeatable calculation path.
C. Hide errors in the final output without checking why they occurred.
D. Start with the business requirement, use the correct feature, and test the output before sharing it.
Correct answer: D
Explanation: Correct use of Lookup functions: VLOOKUP, HLOOKUP, INDEX-MATCH means applying the right Excel capability and checking it against formula outputs, PivotTable totals, source row counts, and sample records. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

Q5. In a GBP 45,000+ analyst role, how should Text functions: CONCATENATE, LEFT, RIGHT, MID, TRIM, TEXT be treated? [Concept]
A. It supports repeatable analysis rather than one-off manual judgement.
B. It is useful only for personal practice files and not for team analytics work.
C. It replaces the need to understand the business question.
D. It is relevant only when the dataset contains fewer than one hundred rows.
Correct answer: A
Explanation: Text functions: CONCATENATE, LEFT, RIGHT, MID, TRIM, TEXT matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

B. Create the output, inspect edge cases, and confirm that the result answers the stakeholder question.
C. Prioritise cosmetic formatting before confirming the metric definition.
D. Share the output immediately and wait for stakeholders to find any issues.
Correct answer: B
Explanation: Correct use of Text functions: CONCATENATE, LEFT, RIGHT, MID, TRIM, TEXT means applying the right Excel capability and checking it against formula outputs, PivotTable totals, source row counts, and sample records. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

B. Use the tool feature deliberately, check a small sample, and document any assumption in the workbook.
C. Prioritise cosmetic formatting before confirming the metric definition.
D. Share the output immediately and wait for stakeholders to find any issues.
Correct answer: B
Explanation: Correct use of Introduction to Power Query means applying the right Excel capability and checking it against formula outputs, PivotTable totals, source row counts, and sample records. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

### Scenario-Based Questions - Intermediate
Q1. You are supporting a UK retail chain and must prepare a monthly performance pack for finance and operations. Several teams disagree about the numbers, and Logical functions: IF, AND, OR, Nested IF is central. What should you do? [Scenario]
A. Delete outliers or missing values before checking what they represent.
B. Agree definitions, profile the source data, build a repeatable solution using Logical functions: IF, AND, OR, Nested IF, validate against source totals, and document caveats.
C. Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
D. Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.
Correct answer: B
Explanation: This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Logical functions: IF, AND, OR, Nested IF, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

Q2. A refreshed deliverable for a London fintech team looks wrong while you compare regional performance where teams use different definitions. The investigation involves Lookup functions: VLOOKUP, HLOOKUP, INDEX-MATCH. What is the strongest approach? [Scenario]
A. Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
B. Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.
C. Trace the data grain, test the transformation or calculation path, use Lookup functions: VLOOKUP, HLOOKUP, INDEX-MATCH deliberately, and review the result with the data owner.
D. Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.
Correct answer: C
Explanation: This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Lookup functions: VLOOKUP, HLOOKUP, INDEX-MATCH, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

Q3. Your manager at an NHS reporting team asks you to combine multiple extracts before a senior stakeholder review with a repeatable process. How should you apply Text functions: CONCATENATE, LEFT, RIGHT, MID, TRIM, TEXT? [Scenario]
A. Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.
B. Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.
C. Optimise the final screen before checking source data grain or joins.
D. Create a reproducible workflow, check edge cases, validate the output, and make the assumptions visible to stakeholders.
Correct answer: D
Explanation: This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Text functions: CONCATENATE, LEFT, RIGHT, MID, TRIM, TEXT, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

Q4. You inherit a partially built workbook for a regional transport operator while trying to investigate why a refreshed dashboard shows unexpected totals. The weak area is Date and time functions. What is the best response? [Scenario]
A. Compare the current result with a trusted baseline, isolate where Date and time functions affects the numbers, and fix the logic transparently.
B. Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.
C. Optimise the final screen before checking source data grain or joins.
D. Assume another team has already validated the extract and skip reconciliation.
Correct answer: A
Explanation: This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Date and time functions, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

Q5. You are supporting a local council analytics team and must standardise a repeatable reporting process across departments. Several teams disagree about the numbers, and Conditional formatting is central. What should you do? [Scenario]

B. Agree definitions, profile the source data, build a repeatable solution using Conditional formatting, validate against source totals, and document caveats.

D. Hide caveats so the final answer looks more confident.

Explanation: This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Conditional formatting, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

## Advanced
### Core MCQs (Concept + Tool-Usage)
Q1. Which statement best explains the role of Dynamic arrays: XLOOKUP, FILTER, SORT, UNIQUE in Excel for UK data analytics work? [Concept]
A. It improves reliability, interpretability, and auditability before decisions are made.
B. It is mainly a visual styling choice with no effect on analytical quality.
C. It guarantees that all source data is correct even when no validation is performed.
D. It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.
Correct answer: A
Explanation: Dynamic arrays: XLOOKUP, FILTER, SORT, UNIQUE matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

Q2. Which practical step best demonstrates correct use of Dynamic arrays: XLOOKUP, FILTER, SORT, UNIQUE in a workbook? [Tool-Usage]
A. Assume the default output is correct and skip all checks against the source.
B. Use the tool feature deliberately, check a small sample, and document any assumption in the workbook.
C. Change every field to text to avoid thinking about data types or aggregation.
D. Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.
Correct answer: B
Explanation: Correct use of Dynamic arrays: XLOOKUP, FILTER, SORT, UNIQUE means applying the right Excel capability and checking it against formula outputs, PivotTable totals, source row counts, and sample records. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

Q3. What is the strongest reason to understand Advanced PivotTables: calculated fields, slicers, timelines before presenting analysis? [Concept]
A. It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.
B. It should be used only after the report has already been published.
C. It reduces ambiguity in definitions, calculations, and assumptions.
D. It is useful only for personal practice files and not for team analytics work.
Correct answer: C
Explanation: Advanced PivotTables: calculated fields, slicers, timelines matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

Q4. Which workflow is the best way to use Advanced PivotTables: calculated fields, slicers, timelines in production-style analysis? [Tool-Usage]
A. Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.
B. Build the answer manually and avoid leaving a repeatable calculation path.
C. Hide errors in the final output without checking why they occurred.
D. Start with the business requirement, use the correct feature, and test the output before sharing it.
Correct answer: D
Explanation: Correct use of Advanced PivotTables: calculated fields, slicers, timelines means applying the right Excel capability and checking it against formula outputs, PivotTable totals, source row counts, and sample records. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

### Scenario-Based Questions - Advanced (complex, multi-step, case-study style)
Q1. You lead an analytics workstream for a UK retail chain and must design an auditable executive reporting workflow under a tight deadline. The solution depends on Dynamic arrays: XLOOKUP, FILTER, SORT, UNIQUE in Excel. What is the best approach? [Scenario]
A. Delete outliers or missing values before checking what they represent.
B. Map requirements and data grain, design the solution around Dynamic arrays: XLOOKUP, FILTER, SORT, UNIQUE, test security and performance, validate with owners, and publish with monitoring.
C. Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
D. Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.
Correct answer: B
Explanation: This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Dynamic arrays: XLOOKUP, FILTER, SORT, UNIQUE, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.
